neighborhoods.name: Church Street
neighborhoods.description: Church Street sits just off Downtown and shares much of its convenience, but at a slightly calmer scale. The mix of apartment buildings, smaller residential blocks, and longtime local institutions gives it a central location with a more settled feel.

image.alt: View along Dr. Martin Luther King Drive in White Plains with the public library in the foreground and civic buildings beyond.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Downtown is the most walkable and built-up part of White Plains, centered on the Metro-North station, Mamaroneck Avenue, restaurants, shopping, and a growing collection of newer apartment buildings. It has the most day-to-night energy in the city and the strongest sense of White Plains as a compact urban center.

image.alt: Street-level view of Downtown White Plains with storefronts, mid-rise buildings, and active sidewalks.

neighborhoods.name: Government Center
neighborhoods.description: Government Center is one of the most central parts of White Plains and gives the surrounding streets an established, civic feel. It is easy to place on the map and easy to move through, with Downtown and several nearby neighborhoods close by.

image.alt: Exterior view of the Westchester County Government complex in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Health Center
neighborhoods.description: Testing.

image.alt: White Plains Hospital buildings seen across Maple Avenue.

neighborhoods.name: Winbrook
neighborhoods.description: Winbrook is close to the center of the city and has long been one of White Plains' better-known residential areas. The Brookfield Commons redevelopment has added new affordable housing and community space, so the neighborhood now combines an established presence with visible new investment.

image.alt: Historic view of the Winbrook Houses in White Plains.
image.credit: Gottscho-Schleisner Collection via Library of Congress and Wikimedia Commons

neighborhoods.name: Gateway
neighborhoods.description: Gateway lives up to its name as the stretch between the city center and the residential neighborhoods around it. It feels connected and convenient, with a little breathing room from the busiest blocks while still staying close to them.

image.alt: Metro-North's White Plains station after the station renewal project.
image.credit: Metropolitan Transportation Authority via Wikimedia Commons

neighborhoods.name: Battle Hill
neighborhoods.description: Test description.

image.alt: Battle of White Plains site marker in Battle of White Plains Park.

neighborhoods.name: Fisher Hill
neighborhoods.description: Fisher Hill is a historic neighborhood within walking distance of the central business district of White Plains, NY. Residential development began around 1900, and many of the large vintage homes from that era still define the neighborhood's character today. Fisher Hill is notably diverse in ethnicity, age, and income.

image.alt: Fisher Hill residential street with stone walls, historic homes, and downtown White Plains towers in the distance.

neighborhoods.name: Fulton Street
neighborhoods.description: Fulton Street is a smaller west-side neighborhood with a long-standing residential feel. It is shaped more by familiar blocks and older housing than by large new development, which gives it a straightforward and established character.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Fulton Street neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Ferris Avenue
neighborhoods.description: Ferris Avenue follows one of the city's main north-south routes and feels closely tied to the surrounding west side. The area mixes housing with local activity, and its main draw is how easily it links Battle Hill, Downtown, and the neighborhoods to the north.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Ferris Avenue neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Bronx River
neighborhoods.description: Bronx River is a narrow edge neighborhood along the western side of White Plains. Its position gives it a tucked-along-the-corridor feel, with easy access to central White Plains while still feeling a little set apart.

image.alt: Historic bridge view along the Bronx River Parkway corridor near White Plains.
image.credit: Gilmore D. Clarke via Wikimedia Commons

neighborhoods.name: Prospect Park
neighborhoods.description: Prospect Park is a small southwest pocket with a calm, local feel. It sits between busier parts of the city, but the neighborhood itself reads as modest, residential, and easygoing.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Prospect Park neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: North Broadway
neighborhoods.description: North Broadway has one of the most recognizable addresses in White Plains, combining a main route through the city with long-established residential stretches. It feels prominent without feeling overly busy, and it carries some of the north side's older character.

neighborhoods.name: Good Counsel
neighborhoods.description: Good Counsel takes much of its identity from the Good Counsel Complex on North Broadway, a White Plains landmark listed on the State and National Registers of Historic Places. That historic campus gives the area a distinctive look and a sense of permanence that sets it apart from the tighter street grid closer to Downtown.

image.alt: The chapel at the Good Counsel Complex in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Westminster Ridge
neighborhoods.description: Westminster Ridge sits just north of the city center and has a quiet hillside feel. It comes across as settled and residential, with a little separation from Downtown while still staying close to it.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Westminster Ridge neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Woodcrest Heights
neighborhoods.description: Woodcrest Heights has a northeast setting that feels more tucked away than the city core. The name fits the area well: it reads as residential, slightly elevated, and a little quieter than the neighborhoods closer to the station.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Woodcrest Heights neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: White Plains Reservoir
neighborhoods.description: White Plains Reservoir occupies the far northern part of the map and feels more open than the middle of the city. Its position gives it a sense of extra space and a more outlying, edge-of-town character.

image.alt: A tranquil 1902 view of St. Mary's Lake (the White Plains municipal reservoir), woods reflecting off the still water.
image.credit: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive

neighborhoods.name: Eastview
neighborhoods.description: Eastview sits east of Downtown and works as one of the city's steady residential neighborhoods. It has a practical, everyday feel, with a location that keeps the center of White Plains and the eastern side of town close by.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Eastview neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Old Oak Ridge
neighborhoods.description: Old Oak Ridge is a small east-side pocket with a tucked-in, private feel. It reads as residential and somewhat apart from the busier blocks closer to the core, while still staying well placed for getting around.

neighborhoods.name: Westchester Avenue
neighborhoods.description: Westchester Avenue is shaped by one of White Plains' main east-west routes, so convenience is a big part of its identity. It feels connected, visible, and easy to place, with quick links to the eastern neighborhoods and the center of the city.

image.alt: Westchester Avenue in White Plains looking west near Bloomingdale Road.

neighborhoods.name: Old Mamaroneck Road
neighborhoods.description: Old Mamaroneck Road feels defined by one of the city's long-running routes. It sits in a useful middle ground between central White Plains and the southeastern residential areas, with a familiar, well-established neighborhood feel.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Old Mamaroneck Road neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Carhart
neighborhoods.description: Carhart is a compact neighborhood area near the middle of White Plains, positioned between the close-in residential blocks, institutional anchors, and larger south-side neighborhoods around it. It is one of the neighborhood associations currently represented through WPCNA, giving residents another point of connection within the council.

neighborhoods.name: Kirkbride Asylum
neighborhoods.description: Kirkbride Asylum has one of the most distinctive names on the map, and the area carries a sense of older institutional history and larger grounds. It feels different from the tighter city blocks nearby, with a more open layout and a character shaped by place as much as by housing.

image.alt: Bloomingdale Hospital (the Kirkbride-plan psychiatric campus) in White Plains, photographed around 1902, showing the half-timbered reception building with its clock tower.
image.credit: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive

neighborhoods.name: Bryant Gardens
neighborhoods.description: Bryant Gardens is best known for the garden-style cooperative community built there in the early 1950s, with landscaped grounds and a strong sense of internal identity. The name still carries that orderly, residential feel, and it remains one of the more recognizable small-scale neighborhoods in White Plains.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Bryant Gardens neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Burke Institute
neighborhoods.description: Burke Institute is anchored by the Burke campus on Mamaroneck Avenue, whose rehabilitation hospital opened in 1915. That longstanding institution gives the area a clear identity and a campus-like feel within the larger residential section around it.

image.alt: Historic postcard view of Burke Hospital in White Plains.
image.credit: Boston Public Library via Wikimedia Commons

neighborhoods.name: Havilands Manor
neighborhoods.description: Havilands Manor has a tucked-in east-side setting and a polished residential feel. It comes across as quiet, well established, and a little removed from the busiest parts of White Plains in a way that gives it its own character.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Havilands Manor neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: North Street
neighborhoods.description: North Street covers a broad stretch of the east and southeast side and is one of the roomier-feeling sections of White Plains. Parts of it are known for larger homes and longer blocks, and the overall impression is residential, established, and spacious.

image.alt: School and campus buildings along North Street in White Plains with an open lawn and neighborhood edge in view.

neighborhoods.name: Gedney Park
neighborhoods.description: Gedney Park sits between the Old Mamaroneck Road corridor and the larger Gedney neighborhoods around it. It feels central within the south side of White Plains, with a settled residential character and easy connections in several directions.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Gedney Park neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Gedney Farms
neighborhoods.description: Gedney Farms is one of the classic residential names in White Plains, with roots that trace back to the 1700s and a neighborhood association now marking its 100th year. It is known for quiet, tree-lined streets, larger homes, and the kind of traditional character that has made it one of the city's most established addresses.

image.alt: Tree-lined residential street in Gedney Farms with large homes, deep front lawns, and a mature canopy in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Gedney Circle
neighborhoods.description: Gedney Circle is small enough to feel like an enclave, which is part of its appeal. The name suggests a clearly defined pocket within the larger Gedney area, with a residential tone and a strong sense of place.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Gedney Circle neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Gedney Commons
neighborhoods.description: Gedney Commons is one of the smaller Gedney sections, compact and closely tied to its surrounding streets. It feels approachable and neighborhood-scaled, with the established look that runs through much of the south side.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Gedney Commons neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Gedney Manor
neighborhoods.description: Gedney Manor has a steady, settled feel and sits close to several of the other Gedney neighborhoods. It reads as residential and long established, with a quieter tone than the city center.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Gedney Manor neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Gedney Meadows
neighborhoods.description: Gedney Meadows has a softer, more tucked-away feel than some of the busier south-side corridors. The area comes across as calm and residential, with a name that fits its gentler character.

image.alt: Aerial view over Gedney Meadows with White Plains homes and trees in the foreground and the downtown skyline in the distance.

neighborhoods.name: Brook Hills
neighborhoods.description: Brook Hills is a small southeastern pocket that feels compact and residential. Its setting near the lower edge of the city gives it a bit of separation, while the neighborhood itself reads as neat and self-contained.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Brook Hills neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Holbrooke
neighborhoods.description: Holbrooke sits among several south-side neighborhoods and feels modest, established, and local. It is the kind of small residential section that tends to come into focus once someone starts learning the White Plains map.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Holbrooke neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Reynal Park
neighborhoods.description: Reynal Park is a small neighborhood with a distinctly local feel. The name gives it a bit of identity of its own, and the area reads as calm, residential, and tucked into the south side.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Reynal Park neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Hillair Circle
neighborhoods.description: Hillair Circle is one of the more clearly enclosed-feeling neighborhoods on the map. That small scale gives it a private, neighborhood-first character that stands out in a city with many larger, looser districts.

image.alt: Panoramic 1902 view from the Hillair area of White Plains, looking across fields and farmland toward the distant hills.
image.credit: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive

neighborhoods.name: Colonial Corners
neighborhoods.description: Colonial Corners is a compact residential section near the southern middle of the city. It feels traditional and familiar, with a straightforward neighborhood presence that fits naturally among the Gedney areas.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Colonial Corners neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Rocky Dell
neighborhoods.description: Rocky Dell is a tucked-away south-side neighborhood with a slightly more hidden feel. Its smaller footprint and position near the edge of the city give it a quiet, off-the-main-route quality.

image.alt: Rocky Dell, the 1902 residence of N.C. Reynal, a Queen Anne-style house on the Rocky Dell estate in White Plains.
image.credit: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive

neighborhoods.name: Soundview
neighborhoods.description: Soundview is a long-established residential neighborhood on the southwest side of White Plains. It has a plainspoken, lived-in feel and comes across as solid, familiar, and easy to understand.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Soundview neighborhood in White Plains.

neighborhoods.name: Rosedale
neighborhoods.description: Rosedale is one of the larger and better-known southern neighborhoods in White Plains. It feels residential, green, and well established, with a calmer pace and a strong sense of continuity from block to block.

image.alt: Aerial view across Rosedale in White Plains with curving residential streets, treetops, and homes spreading across the neighborhood.

neighborhoods.name: Saxon Woods
neighborhoods.description: Saxon Woods sits at the southern edge of the city near Saxon Woods Park, whose golf course, trails, pool, and recreation areas give this part of White Plains a greener identity. The neighborhood feels more open than the center and carries some of the outdoors-oriented appeal of the park beside it.

image.alt: Historic view of the Saxon Woods Bathing Pool in White Plains.
image.credit: Gottscho-Schleisner Collection via Library of Congress and Wikimedia Commons

neighborhoods.name: Idle Forest
neighborhoods.description: Idle Forest is one of the quieter-sounding names on the map, and the area matches that impression. It feels tucked in and residential, with a softer, lower-profile presence than some of the more prominent White Plains neighborhoods.

image.alt: Stylized hero illustration for the Idle Forest neighborhood in White Plains.
